name: Lemon Roasted Chicken
tagline: Whole chicken, lemon halves, garlic, thyme — high heat, golden skin
highlight: Crackly lemon-perfumed skin, juicy thighs, no nuts anywhere

hero.alt: Golden roast chicken with charred lemon halves and thyme
hero.caption: lemon roast chicken — golden, herby

healthFacts: Naturally nut-free — only chicken, citrus, herbs, and olive oil.
A 4-lb chicken yields ~120g of cooked protein per quarter portion.
Roasting at high heat (425°F) renders fat outward and crisps the skin without drying the breast.

steps.title: Dry-brine the bird
steps.detail: Pat chicken bone-dry with paper towels. Salt all over and inside the cavity. Rest uncovered in the fridge 30 minutes — this is what gets you crisp skin.
steps.tip: If you have time, dry-brine overnight. The skin only gets crisper.

media.alt: Salted whole chicken resting on a rack
media.caption: dry-brine the bird

steps.title: Stuff and oil
steps.detail: Halve the lemons and the garlic head. Stuff cavity with lemon halves, garlic, and thyme sprigs. Rub the bird with olive oil and a generous grind of pepper.
steps.tip: Tie the legs together with kitchen twine — it makes the breast cook more evenly.

media.alt: Chicken cavity stuffed with lemon and garlic
media.caption: stuff and oil

steps.title: Roast hot
steps.detail: Roast breast-side-up at 425°F (220°C) for 55–65 minutes, until thigh meat reads 165°F (74°C). The lemons in the cavity will steam-perfume the meat from the inside.
steps.tip: Pan looking dry? Pour a half cup of water in the bottom partway through to keep drippings from burning.

media.alt: Golden roast chicken in a cast iron pan
media.caption: roast hot

steps.title: Rest and carve
steps.detail: Tent loosely with foil and rest 15 minutes — non-negotiable. Carve, squeeze a roasted lemon half over, and serve with the pan juices.
steps.tip: Cutting into a hot bird flushes out the juices. Patience = juicy chicken.

utensils.name: Roasting pan or cast iron skillet
